segments.text: Expensive hotels always trap their showers inside a clear glass box. But in our homes, the bathroom is usually just a normal open room. It looks like a
fancy design choice for the hotels, but it is actually solving some massive problems. See, at home, we just use simple shower curtains or keep it open.
But for a hotel, curtains look cheap. They clink to you and they grow mold.
While a glass box instantly feels premium [music] and is much faster for their staff to clean, why not just build a solid wall? Because a solid wall
blocks the light, it makes the shower feels like a tiny dark cave. Glass lets the light flow naturally, making the whole washroom look incredibly huge. But
here is a real secret. Why enclose the shower at all? Why not just leave the bathroom completely open? Because the water ruins everything. The glass
creates a strict wet zone. It stops water from splashing onto your wash basin, rotting the expensive wooden cabinets, and turning your floor into a
dangerous sleep hazard. It keeps everything clean and saves so much of money. For more design secrets, subscribe.
